caption_en: Table 5. Cross-validation of CHIRPS and ERA5-Land against the two recent INAMHI automatic stations (M0105 Otavalo and M1240 Ibarra-INAMHI); 4 station-product pairs (CHIRPS–M1240 and CHIRPS–M0105 for monthly precipitation; ERA5-Land–M1240 and ERA5-Land–M0105 for monthly mean temperature). Overlap windows: CHIRPS 2014–2025 (M1240; n = 89) and 2019–2024 (M0105; n = 64); ERA5-Land 2014–2025 (both stations; n = 97 and 89). Metrics: monthly observations (n_meses), Pearson correlation, RMSE, MAE, mean bias and relative bias (%), regression slope. Status thresholds: ACCEPTABLE = r ≥ 0.60 AND |relative bias| ≤ 50 %; LIMITED = 0.40 ≤ r < 0.60 OR 50 % < |relative bias| ≤ 100 %; REJECTED = r < 0.40 OR |relative bias| > 100 %. Resulting labels: CHIRPS–M1240 ACCEPTABLE; CHIRPS–M0105 LIMITED; ERA5-Land–M1240 and ERA5-Land–M0105 REJECTED (systematic warm bias of +8.7 to +9.3 °C over the Imbabura inter-Andean valley, as expected from the ~9-km grid over complex topography). The 1994–2013 INAMHI yearbook block is used as a documentary reference for the climatological in-situ records (§4.1) but does not enter this cross-validation, since the recent automatic block is the only one with hourly-derived observations contemporaneous with the gridded products at the same processing standard.

rows.Status: REJECTED — systematic warm bias ERA5 (+8.7°C in Andes)
REJECTED — systematic warm bias ERA5 (+8.7°C in Andes)
